content.problemStatement:
There are NNN hidden integer arrays of length NNN each. You are given the mex of each of these NNN arrays.

Ashish likes lower bounds and Kryptonix likes upper bounds. So, for each 0≤i≤N−10 \leq i \leq N-10≤i≤N−1, find:


The least possible number of occurrences of iii across all the arrays
The most possible number of occurrences of iii across all the arrays
Note that these values must be computed independently, i.e, it is allowed to choose different configurations of arrays to attempt to minimize/maximize the number of occurrences of different integers.

Please see the samples for an example.

Recall that the mex of an array is the smallest non-negative integer that is not present in it. For example, the mex of [0,2,4,1,1,5][0, 2, 4, 1, 1, 5][0,2,4,1,1,5] is 333, and the mex of [3,8,101,99,100][3, 8, 101, 99, 100][3,8,101,99,100] is 000.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the mexes of the NNN arrays.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines, each containing two space-separated integers. The iii-th line should contain the least and the most possible number of occurrences of iii across all the arrays.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053\cdot10^53⋅105
